record.field: Penpot is the open-source design and prototyping platform that unites designers and developers around open web standards.

Penpot is the open-source design and prototyping platform that unites designers and developers around open web standards.

Penpot lets product teams design interfaces and prototypes collaboratively in the browser, storing work as standard SVG/CSS/HTML rather than a proprietary format. Built natively on web technologies, it offers real CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts, built-in design tokens, and a developer inspect mode that exposes ready-to-use code. It is the only major design platform that can be fully self-hosted, making it a strong fit for teams that need data ownership and no vendor lock-in.

Key features
  • Real-time collaborative design and interactive prototyping in the browser
  • Native CSS Grid and Flexbox layouts that map directly to shipped code
  • Built-in design tokens as a single source of truth between design and development
  • Developer mode with instant access to SVG, CSS and HTML output
  • Open API, plugin system and MCP server for automation and AI-driven workflows
  • Fully self-hostable via Docker or Kubernetes under the MPL-2.0 license
Best for

Design and product teams, agencies and privacy-conscious organizations that want a self-hosted, standards-based alternative to proprietary design tools, with tight design-to-code collaboration and full control over their design infrastructure.

services: penpot-frontend: image: penpotapp/frontend:2.6.1 restart: always ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:8080' volumes: - ./penpot_assets:/opt/data/assets depends_on: - penpot-backend - penpot-exporter environment: PENPOT_FLAGS: disable-email-verification enable-smtp enable-prepl-server disable-secure-session-cookies container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network penpot-backend: image: penpotapp/backend:2.6.1 restart: always volumes: - ./penpot_assets:/opt/data/assets depends_on: - penpot-postgres - penpot-redis environment: PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI: http://{{ domain_name }} PENPOT_FLAGS: disable-email-verification enable-smtp enable-prepl-server disable-secure-session-cookies PENPOT_DATABASE_URI: postgresql://penpot-postgres/penpot PENPOT_DATABASE_USERNAME: penpot PENPOT_DATABASE_PASSWORD: penpot PENPOT_REDIS_URI: redis://penpot-redis/0 PENPOT_ASSETS_STORAGE_BACKEND: assets-fs PENPOT_STORAGE_ASSETS_FS_DIRECTORY: /opt/data/assets PENPOT_TELEMETRY_ENABLED: true PENPOT_SMTP_DEFAULT_FROM: no-reply@example.com PENPOT_SMTP_DEFAULT_REPLY_TO: no-reply@example.com PENPOT_SMTP_HOST: penpot-mailcatch PENPOT_SMTP_PORT: 1025 PENPOT_SMTP_USERNAME: null PENPOT_SMTP_PASSWORD: null PENPOT_SMTP_TLS: false PENPOT_SMTP_SSL: false container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_penpot_backend' networks: - internal penpot-exporter: image: penpotapp/exporter:2.6.1 restart: always environment: PENPOT_PUBLIC_URI: http://penpot-frontend PENPOT_REDIS_URI: redis://penpot-redis/0 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_penpot_exporter' networks: - internal penpot-postgres: image: postgres:15 restart: always stop_signal: SIGINT volumes: - ./penpot_postgres_v15:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: - POSTGRES_INITDB_ARGS=--data-checksums - POSTGRES_DB=penpot - POSTGRES_USER=penpot - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=penpot container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_penpot_postgres' networks: - internal penpot-redis: image: redis:7.2 restart: always container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_penpot_redis' networks: - internal penpot-mailcatch: image: sj26/mailcatcher:latest restart: always expose: - '1025' ports: - 1080 container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_penpot_mailcatch' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T penpot-postgres sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d penpot-postgres for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T penpot-postgres sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T penpot-postgres sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T penpot-postgres sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
